_comment: U-V.4 (11-verification #6). Each entry maps one expected shadow-parity diff class to an audit finding. matches a shadow row when legacy_blocked/engine_blocked equal the row's canonicalized values AND (engine_error_class is null OR equals the row's engine.error_class). Entries are added by the U-R.* unit that introduces the intentional divergence -- see each execution pack's 'Tests' section for the audit finding id to cite.

entries.note: row-count based, closes the per-call quantity bypass legacy's JSON-entry count missed
E->VF: engine allows a board-less CPU add in draft where legacy hard-blocked at every trigger
three legacy implementations (validateMemorySlotAvailability, a per-call-count check, MemoryAuthority) unified into one row-count based check
unknown/unparseable card width now blocks (pcie.unknown_width); legacy added the card without a slot and only logged a warning
PCIE_LANE_CHECK_ENABLED defaults to warn (never blocks) in production; the engine rule always enforces via the single ledger-based model, independent of that legacy flag
legacy's M.2-over-population check only runs at read-time (getConfigurationWarnings), never at add; the engine rule blocks at ADD

_note_system_psu_capacity_v4: system.psu_capacity (U-R.7, closes audit V-4) triggers only VALIDATE+FINALIZE, never ADD -- the only live shadow hook today (ServerBuilder::validateComponentAddition) evaluates exclusively under Trigger::ADD (U-V.3's single hook site), so this rule can never appear in a shadow row yet, matching cpu.requires_board's/storage.caddy_pairing's own non-diff structural reasoning. It becomes shadow-observable once U-C.5's TransitionStatusCommand (or a future VALIDATE-triggered hook) runs in shadow mode.
_note_system_inventory_state_v2: system.inventory_state (U-R.7, closes audit V-2) triggers only VALIDATE+FINALIZE -- same non-observability reasoning as system.psu_capacity above. Legacy's own V-2 behavior (validateConfiguration() appending a non-blocking issue on Status===0) only runs inside validate-config/finalize paths anyway, never at add, so there would be no ADD-side legacy verdict to diff against even if the hook did fire there.
_note_system_singleton: system.singleton (U-R.7, closes audit A-5/D3) DOES trigger ADD, unlike its two U-R.7 siblings above -- but legacy's own motherboard/chassis duplicate-add block lives in ServerBuilder::addComponent() itself (~4236, 'Configuration already has a motherboard'), OUTSIDE legacyValidateComponentAddition() (the method the ADD-trigger shadow hook actually wraps). Whether this produces a real shadow diff on a real duplicate-motherboard-add fixture was not traced to certainty this session (would need a live/scratch-DB shadow run to observe) -- deliberately left WITHOUT a fabricated matcher entry so a real occurrence surfaces as an unexplained diff for human review, same posture as storage.interface_path's SAS-backplane gap.
_note_dependency_blocked_removal: dependency.blocked_removal (U-R.8, closes audit R-1) triggers REMOVE+REPLACE. Per the U-R.8 pack itself: 'REMOVE ops have no legacy engine hook yet (arrives U-C.3) -- unit tests are the gate here.' U-C.3 (this session, same batch) added RemoveComponentCommand with a dryRun()-based shadow hook in server_api.php's handleRemoveComponent, logging to reports/shadow/command-<Ymd>.jsonl -- a DIFFERENT JSONL stream than ShadowRunner's ENGINE_MODE-keyed reports/shadow/engine-<Ymd>.jsonl this file's entries describe, and not yet consumed by parity_report.php (which is scoped to the ENGINE_MODE stream). Extending parity_report.php to also read the command-layer shadow stream is out of this unit's box; flagged for a follow-up unit or human decision.
_note_pcie_slot_placement_a7: A-7 (manual slot honored) is not a shadow-observable diff: the live ADD hook (ServerBuilder::validateComponentAddition) does not thread a manual slot request into TargetState yet (arrives with the command layer, U-C.2), so SlotPlanner always plans in auto mode when driven by the shadow hook today. A-7 is proven by SlotPlanner's own unit test (tests/unit/rules/slot_rules_test.php), not by a parity_report row.
_note_storage_caddy_pairing: storage.caddy_pairing is VALIDATION_FAILURE severity, whose triggers() list includes ADD but which (per Verdict::blocking()) only actually contributes to blocking() under VALIDATE/FINALIZE, never ADD. The shadow hook only ever evaluates the ADD trigger (U-V.3's single hook site), so this rule can never disagree with legacy's own never-blocks-at-add behavior there -- no expected_diffs entry needed for the same structural reason as cpu.requires_board's non-diff cases. It will need one once a VALIDATE-triggered hook exists (tracked for U-C.5/finalize wiring).
_note_storage_interface_path: storage.interface_path's SAS-without-HBA check is a deliberate SIMPLIFICATION of legacy's real path search (see the rule's own docblock) -- it does not detect a chassis SAS backplane, which legacy's full StorageConnectionValidator::validate() does. This is a data-dependent divergence with no clean legacy_blocked/engine_blocked matcher (it depends on whether a real chassis fixture declares SAS backplane support), so it is deliberately left WITHOUT an expected_diffs entry: any real occurrence should surface as an unexplained diff for human review rather than being silently pre-approved.
_note_cpu_mixed_models: cpu.mixed_models is WARNING severity, which never contributes to Verdict::blocking() (see core/models/validation/Verdict.php) -- it can never produce a legacy_blocked!==engine_blocked row by construction, so it needs no expected_diffs entry despite being a new-firing rule (RULE_MAP.md U-R.1 row 3, previously orphaned legacy code). Its firings are visible in parity_report's rule_coverage counts instead.
